display.purpose: Closing CTA heading and footer wordmark only, once per page.

body.purpose: Paragraph copy inside service descriptions and legal pages.

label.purpose: Eyebrows, pill labels, form field labels.

motion.name: ease-soft-overshoot
motion.purpose: Default hover/interaction easing for pills, tags, and panel transitions.

motion.name: ease-overshoot-spring
motion.purpose: Card and button hover scale where a touch of overshoot reads as tactile (work cards, contact button, magnetic tags).

components.name: Primary Pill Button (dark)
components.description: Dark pill CTA with an embedded icon well, e.g. the menu toggle and main contact CTA.
components.css: .ds-pill { display: flex; align-items: center; padding: 0.3rem; border-radius: 999px; transition: transform 0.5s cubic-bezier(0.22, 0.68, 0, 1); } .ds-pill:hover, .ds-pill:focus-visible { transform: scale(1.1); } .ds-pill-dark { background: #0c1016; } .ds-pill-label { font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 500; text-transform: uppercase; padding: 0 1rem 0 1.75rem; color: #f0f6f8; } .ds-pill-icon { display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; width: 2rem; height: 2rem; border-radius: 999px; background: #1e232c; color: #f0f6f8; } .ds-pill-icon svg { width: 40%; }

components.name: Light Pill Button
components.description: Soft-grey pill used on dark contexts as the secondary action.
components.css: .ds-pill { display: flex; align-items: center; padding: 0.3rem; border-radius: 999px; transition: transform 0.5s cubic-bezier(0.22, 0.68, 0, 1); } .ds-pill:hover, .ds-pill:focus-visible { transform: scale(1.1); } .ds-pill-light { background: #d9e0e3; } .ds-pill-label { font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 500; text-transform: uppercase; padding: 0 1rem 0 1.75rem; color: #000000; } .ds-pill-icon { display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; width: 2rem; height: 2rem; border-radius: 999px; background: #f0f6f8; color: #000000; } .ds-pill-icon svg { width: 40%; }

components.name: Stepped Service Card
components.description: Service/pricing card whose background is one step of the grey ramp, with a matching darker chip set inside.
components.html:
UI/UXDesign

  • UI/UX Design
  • Design Systems
components.css: .ds-card { border-radius: 2rem; padding: 3rem 1.5rem; display: flex; flex-direction: column; justify-content: space-between; gap: 4rem; } .ds-card-title { margin: 0; display: flex; flex-direction: column; font-size: 3rem; line-height: 1; letter-spacing: -0.05em; } .ds-card-title-main { color: #f0f6f8; } .ds-card-title-sub { color: #767c7e; } .ds-card-tags { margin: 0; padding: 0; list-style: none; display: flex; flex-wrap: wrap; gap: 0.5rem; } .ds-card-tags li { color: #f0f6f8; font-size: 0.875rem; padding: 0.5rem 1rem; border-radius: 999px; }

components.name: Text Input
components.description: Form field used on the contact page's start-project form.
components.css: .ds-field { display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 0.5rem; text-align: left; } .ds-field-label { font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 600; color: #0c1016; } .ds-field-input { padding: 0.75rem 1rem; border-radius: 0.75rem; border: none; background: #f0f6f8; color: #0c1016; font: inherit; } .ds-field-input:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #0c1016; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Off-canvas Nav Menu
components.description: Soft-grey rounded panel of stacked pill nav links, opened from the header menu toggle.
components.html: HomeStudioWorkContact
components.css: .ds-menu { display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 0.5rem; padding: 1rem; min-width: 12rem; border-radius: 1.5rem; background: #d9e0e3; } .ds-menu-link { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 1rem; padding: 0.25rem 1.5rem; border-radius: 1rem; font-size: 1.875rem; font-weight: 600; letter-spacing: -0.075em; color: #0c1016; transition: background-color 0.2s ease-out; } .ds-menu-link:hover, .ds-menu-link:focus-visible { background: #f0f6f8; }

narrative.overview: Omiko's visitors are local service-business owners, on their phone, comparing this agency against several others in open tabs. They are not impressed by noise — they are impressed by a site that visibly has its act together. The system is built on a strict near-monochrome palette (off-white paper, near-black ink, a tight band of greys) that refuses to reach for color as a crutch. Confidence is expressed through precision: tight negative letter-spacing on display type, exact optical alignment of the wordmark, deliberate scroll-reveal choreography, and tonal stepping in place of drop shadows. Nothing here is decorative; everything that appears is load-bearing. This system explicitly rejects the generic template-agency look: stock photography, interchangeable icon-grid service cards, and vague "we build beautiful websites" filler copy. Every section instead leads with a direct, specific claim ("Simple packages. No surprises.", "Ideas, given real shape.") and lets restrained type and motion carry the credibility.
keyCharacteristics: Two-color spine (paper-white / ink-black) with a narrow grey ramp; color is the exception, not the rule.
Display type is heavy weight (900) with aggressively negative letter-spacing (-0.05em to -0.075em) — confident, not loud.
Depth comes from flat tonal stepping between panels, never from box-shadow.
Pill shapes (999px radius) for every interactive control — buttons, nav, tags, social icons — used consistently as the one shape language.
Motion is subtle and physical: scroll-reveals, magnetic hover scales, never bounce or elastic.

rules.name: The Two-Tone Spine Rule
rules.body: Every screen is built from paper-white and ink-black first; greys exist only to step between them. If a new component needs a third hue, that's a sign it belongs in the services-cloud exception, not the core system.

rules.name: Resolved: the former services-cloud exception
rules.body: The studio page's services-cloud tags previously broke the two-tone spine with six saturated colors. All six now share a single neutral background (Panel Step 1, #23272d) with paper-white text, differentiated only by label. No remaining color exception.

rules.name: The Negative Tracking Rule
rules.body: Anything above body size pulls letter-spacing tight (-0.02em at minimum, -0.075em at the tightest on headlines) — never set wide or default tracking on a heading. Stay above the -0.04em ceiling so letters never visually touch.

rules.name: The Flat-By-Default Rule
rules.body: Surfaces never cast a shadow. If a new component needs to read as 'above' another, step it to a lighter or darker tone in the existing grey ramp instead of reaching for box-shadow.

dos: Do keep the palette to paper-white, ink-black, and the grey ramp; let restraint and precision (tracking, spacing, motion timing) carry differentiation instead of color.
Do use pill shape (999px) for every new interactive control to stay consistent with the existing button/chip/nav language.
Do convey card hierarchy through tonal stepping in the existing grey ramp, not through box-shadow.
Do keep headline tracking tight (-0.02em to -0.075em) and never let a display heading exceed the 12rem ceiling at the largest breakpoint.
Do write short, direct, jargon-free copy — the existing voice ("Simple packages. No surprises.") is the bar for every new section.
Do design mobile-first and verify on a phone viewport before checking desktop, since the primary visitor is comparing agencies on their phone.
donts: Don't introduce stock photography, generic icon-grid service cards, or vague "we build beautiful websites" copy.
Don't add box-shadows for depth; this system is flat-by-default and conveys depth through tone alone.
Don't extend the services-cloud's saturated six-color tag palette to any new component.
Don't use border-left or border-right colored stripes as accents anywhere in this system.
Don't apply gradient text for general emphasis; the one existing use is a narrow, intentional animated sweep on a single word per section.
Don't use bounce or elastic easing; motion stays in the ease-out-quart/quint family already established.
